articles.title: Siblings on a Big Race Show
articles.summary: Katie and Harrison are siblings who joined a TV show called Race Across the World. In this show, teams travel across Europe and Asia without phones and with little money to win a prize. Katie was nervous at first, but Harrison convinced her to go. They had to do jobs to earn more money and faced challenges like getting lost. Katie says spending time with her brother was easy because they know each other well. She thinks being mean to each other actually makes their relationship stronger. The show is fun to watch and helps siblings bond.

articles.title: The Surprise Subscription Story
articles.summary: Neha thought she paid once for a CV builder online, but later found out she was signed up for a monthly subscription without knowing it. Over two years, the company took over £500 from her bank account. She tried to cancel and get her money back, but it was hard because the company is based in America. Many people face similar problems with subscriptions they forget about. The government is trying to stop these tricky subscriptions. Companies should be clear about their rules, but sometimes they are not, making it easy for them to keep taking money. It's important to check bank statements regularly to avoid surprises.

articles.title: Blake Lively's Lawsuit Against Justin Baldoni: What Happened in Court
articles.summary: A judge has dismissed most of the claims in a lawsuit filed by actress Blake Lively against her co-star Justin Baldoni. The lawsuit started in 2024 when Lively accused Baldoni of harassment and trying to ruin her reputation while they worked together on a movie called It Ends with Us. The judge threw out 10 out of 13 allegations, including harassment and defamation, but kept three claims: breach of contract, retaliation, and aiding in retaliation. These three claims will go to a trial in New York in May. Lively's lawyer says the case is about retaliation and damage to her reputation, not just harassment. Baldoni denied the claims and had his own lawsuit against Lively dismissed last year. The case involves their interactions on the movie set, where Lively played a character named Lily Bloom.

articles.title: Harry Clark Meets the Pope to Find Faith
articles.summary: Harry Clark, who won a TV show called The Traitors, went to Rome to meet the Pope. He made a documentary for the BBC about his trip. Harry wanted to talk to the Pope because he had questions about his Catholic faith. He felt sad and lost after leaving the Army and losing friends. His mom told him to pray, which helped him feel better. Meeting the Pope was hard to arrange, but Harry tried calling and emailing until he got to go. In the meeting, they chatted about mental health and funny internet pictures called memes. This experience changed Harry's life and made him happier.

articles.title: Jamie Bell Joins New Peaky Blinders Show
articles.summary: A new Peaky Blinders TV series is coming, and actor Jamie Bell will star in it. The show is about a crime family in Birmingham, England, and this new story happens in the 1950s, ten years after the last movie. Jamie Bell will play Duke Shelby, who is the oldest son of Tommy Shelby from the original series. Other actors like Charlie Heaton, Jessica Brown Findlay, and Lashana Lynch are also joining the cast. The series will show how World War Two affected Birmingham and follow Duke as he becomes more ambitious and dangerous. It will be filmed in Birmingham and have two series with six episodes each. You can watch it on BBC iPlayer, BBC One, and Netflix. The creator, Steven Knight, is excited about this new era for Peaky Blinders.

articles.title: Super-Fast Light Internet for Kids
articles.summary: Scientists have made a new way to connect devices to the internet using light instead of radio waves. This light system is very fast, reaching 360 gigabits per second, which is much quicker than Wi-Fi. It uses tiny lasers on a small chip to send data through the air. This helps in places like homes and schools where many people need fast internet at the same time. The light beams are shaped so they don't interfere with each other, making connections reliable. This new technology uses half the energy of Wi-Fi, which is better for the environment. It could be used in the future for things like video calls and virtual reality without slowing down.
